description.problem: Off-the-shelf LLMs underperform on domain-specific tasks, and traditional fine-tuning requires expensive high-VRAM hardware that's inaccessible for most practitioners.
description.decision: Built a modular QLoRA + PEFT fine-tuning framework for Llama 3 and Mistral, with 4-bit NF4 quantisation (75% VRAM reduction), Flash Attention 2, paged AdamW, and W&B experiment tracking. Supports Alpaca, ShareGPT, and OpenAI instruction formats out of the box.
description.impact: Enables full fine-tuning runs on a single consumer GPU. Ships with a benchmarking suite (perplexity, ROUGE, LLM-as-judge) and production merge/deployment scripts to take adapters straight to serving.

description.problem: Single-vector search in RAG systems suffers poor recall on long-tail queries and high hallucination rates on enterprise documentation — making them unreliable for production use.
description.decision: Designed an agentic RAG pipeline using LangGraph, hybrid retrieval (ChromaDB vector search + BM25 keyword matching), and cross-encoder re-ranking. The agent decides whether to answer from the internal knowledge base or fall back to a general LLM response. Supports both OpenAI GPT-4 and Anthropic Claude 3.5.
description.impact: Production-ready system with multi-format ingestion (PDF, Markdown), FastAPI serving with validation and logging, and measurably fewer hallucinations through the re-ranking layer.

description.problem: ML teams lack a standardised, observable framework for taking models from experiment to production — leading to inconsistent deployments and zero visibility into model behaviour in production.
description.decision: Built a complete MLOps reference implementation covering automated training pipelines, model serialisation, a production FastAPI inference layer, CI/CD via GitHub Actions, containerisation with multi-stage Docker, and real-time Prometheus metrics (latency, request volumes).
description.impact: End-to-end template for the full ML lifecycle in a containerised environment. Observability layer surfaces latency and throughput metrics for every inference request without additional instrumentation.
